_comment_policy: --- Catalog policy: every entry must be a (1) PUBLIC, ungated HF repo (no `huggingface-cli login` required), and (2) QUANTIZED for inference unless the model is so small that quantization adds no value (sub-1 GB embedders/rerankers stay at native precision for retrieval quality). Naming: `:4bit` and `:8bit` only — no `:awq`/`:gptq`/`:bnb`/`:w4a16` aliases. The 4-bit suffix transparently aliases the best ungated 4-bit format available (AWQ > GPTQ-Int4 > compressed-tensors W4A16 > Intel AutoRound > unsloth bnb-4bit). SGLang auto-detects the loader from the repo's config.json — no per-key plumbing needed. `:6bit` is intentionally absent: 6-bit weight quantization is an MLX/GGUF concept; the safetensors ecosystem standardises on 4-bit and 8-bit. ---
_comment_qwen3: --- Qwen 3 (April 2025). All published officially by Alibaba on the Qwen org — AWQ for 4-bit, FP8/GPTQ-Int8 for 8-bit. ---
_comment_qwen35: --- Qwen 3.5 (Sept 2025). Alibaba publishes only base weights ungated; AWQ variants are gated and have no community mirror. We use RedHatAI W4A16/W8A8 for 4B and 9B (the most common sizes), and Intel int4-AutoRound (GPTQ-compatible serialization, loaded via SGLang's GPTQ backend) for 2B and 27B where RedHatAI hasn't shipped yet. ---
_comment_qwen25: --- Qwen 2.5 (text Instruct chat). Qwen publishes AWQ (4-bit) and GPTQ-Int8 for every chat size. ---
_comment_coder: --- Code-specialised LLMs. Qwen2.5-Coder: AWQ + GPTQ-Int8 official. Qwen3-Coder-Next: FP8 official (8-bit) + Intel AutoRound (4-bit). ---
_comment_llama: --- Meta Llama via ungated community quants (the `meta-llama/*` originals are licence-gated). 4-bit prefers AWQ (`hugging-quants/*-AWQ-INT4`) for inference speed; bnb-4bit (`unsloth/*-bnb-4bit`) is the fallback for sizes/variants where AWQ isn't published. 8-bit uses FP8-Dynamic (`unsloth/*-FP8-Dynamic`) on Llama 3.3 (only one with a public FP8 build). ---
_comment_gemma: --- Google Gemma 3 via `unsloth/*-unsloth-bnb-4bit` (`google/gemma-3-*` originals are gated). Unsloth-tuned BitsAndBytes 4-bit; SGLang loads via `--quantization bitsandbytes`. Slower than AWQ but the only ungated 4-bit option for this family. ---
_comment_phi: --- Microsoft Phi-4 via `unsloth/*-unsloth-bnb-4bit`. Phi-4 originals are public on the `microsoft` org but only at full precision (~28 GB for the 14 B); the unsloth bnb-4bit mirrors are what makes them practical on consumer GPUs. ---
_comment_minimax: --- MiniMax (very large MoE — multi-GPU required; will OOM on single 16 GB cards). Quants are RedHatAI compressed-tensors for the M2.5 generation; M2 / M2.7 have no public quant mirror yet. ---
_comment_vlm: --- Vision-language models (served by SGLang with the matching `--chat-template`). Qwen2.5-VL: AWQ official (4-bit). Qwen3-VL: 4-bit via `unsloth/*-bnb-4bit`, 8-bit via `Qwen/*-FP8` official. ---
_comment_embed: --- Embedding models. Kept at native precision: every entry here is <2 GB at fp16/bf16, and 4-bit weight quantization measurably degrades retrieval recall@k. `qwen3-embed:8b` has a RedHatAI 8-bit mirror available as `:8bit`. ---
_comment_rerank: --- Re-rankers. Kept at native precision for the same reason as embeddings (small models, ranking quality sensitive to quantization). Qwen3-Reranker is decoder-based and works on SGLang; bge / jina rerankers are encoder-only with experimental SGLang support. ---
